servinfo.title: Front Suspension Frame, Stabilizer Bar and Bushing Removal and Installation

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Front Suspension Frame and Stabilizer Bar Components

item.ptxt: When installing front suspension frame, support the frame on transmission jack at well-balanced position in the center section of front suspension frame. Improperly supported frame could drop and cause personal injury.
Make sure exhaust system is cool before starting service operation, in order to avoid burn by hot exhaust system components.
When removing and installing suspension arm, be careful not to damage dust cover of suspension arm joint by drive shaft dust cover and brake dust cover.

s1.ptxt: Disconnect negative (–) cable at battery.
Disconnect steering lower shaft from pinion shaft.
Hoist vehicle and remove front wheels.
Remove engine undercover.
Remove muffler mounting from front suspension frame.
Disconnect stabilizer joint from stabilizer bar.
Disconnect tie-rod end from steering knuckle.
Remove suspension arm.
Remove propeller shaft.
Support engine assembly using chain hoist (1) or engine crane.

attention3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage hood with lifting device.

s1.ptxt: Remove engine rear mounting bolt (1).
Support front suspension frame (2) using jack (1).
Remove suspension frame mounting No.1 bolt (1) and No.2 bolt (2), then lower jack to remove suspension frame (3) together with stabilizer bar and steering gear case from vehicle body.
Remove steering gear case assembly from front suspension frame.
Remove front stabilizer bar (1) and bushing from front suspension frame (2).

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Front Stabilizer Bar, Bushing and Joint Check

s1.ptxt: Install front stabilizer bar (1), bushing (2) and mounting brackets (3) to front suspension frame as shown in figure.

item.ptxt: Install stabilizer bar (1) with identification painting (5) facing backward.
Install stabilizer bar bushing (2) with its end closely fitted against ring (4) of stabilizer bar.

s1.ptxt: Tighten stabilizer bar mounting bracket bolts to specified torque in numerical order of (“1” and “2”) as shown in figure.
Install steering gear case assembly to front suspension frame.
Put front suspension frame (2) on jack (1) and raise jack.
Align right and left bosses (1) of front suspension frame with holes (2) in vehicle body.
Install new suspension frame mounting No.1 bolt (1) and No.2 bolt (2) and tighten them to specified torques.
Lower jack.
Install engine rear mounting bolt to specified torque.
Remove chain hoist from engine assembly.
Install propeller shaft.
Install suspension arms to front suspension frame, then finger tighten new suspension arm front bushing bolt and suspension arm rear bushing bolt temporarily by hand.
Connect suspension arm joint to steering knuckle.
Connect tie-rod end to steering knuckle.
Connect stabilizer joint to stabilizer bar.
Install muffler mounting to front suspension frame.
Install engine undercover.
Install front wheels and tighten wheel nuts to specified torque.
Connect steering lower shaft to pinion shaft.
Lower hoist and bounce vehicle up and down several times to stabilize suspension. And then, tighten suspension arm front bolt and rear bolt to specified torque.
Connect negative (–) cable at battery.
Check front wheel alignment.
